title: ricinocarpus bisetosus
intro: Ricinocarpus bisetosus, also known as the Himalayan arrowroot or Indian arrowroot, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia. It is a type of medicinal herb that has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipruritic properties. The plant's tubers and roots are rich in starch, which can be used to make herbal preparations that help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The plant's medicinal properties are often utilized in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and digestive issues.
